title: NOC 41401 – Economists and Economic Policy Researchers and Analysts | GTR Immigration
description: Economists and Economic Policy Researchers and Analysts under NOC 41401 are highly skilled professionals who analyze economic data, develop forecasting models, and advise governments and organizations on fiscal, monetary, and trade policies. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role plays a strategic part in Canada’s economic planning and financial systems. These professionals work across public institutions, financial sectors, research organizations, and consulting firms, contributing to national and regional economic development.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law and social, community and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41401

duties: Economists and Economic Policy Researchers perform some or all of the following duties:
Conduct economic research and develop analytical models
Prepare income, expenditure, interest rate, and exchange rate forecasts
Advise government agencies on economic policies
Analyze labour market trends, wages, and unemployment
Apply statistical techniques and econometric models
Study monetary systems and financial institutions
Analyze corporate financial performance and production costs
Examine trade data and international exchange patterns
Forecast renewable and non-renewable resource supply and demand
Conduct market research and pricing analysis
Support regulatory processes and legal proceedings
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in economics or related discipline (business administration, statistics, finance) usually required
Doctorate in economics may be required for senior research or academic roles
Experience with data modeling and statistical software often required
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Economists and Economic Policy Researchers typically earn between:
$57,700 – $138,200 per year, depending on sector, specialization, and seniority.
Senior government economists, investment economists, and energy economists may earn significantly higher compensation.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41401 is stable and competitive due to:
Demand remains strong in finance, energy, public policy, and research sectors.
labour_market_demand: Economists and Policy Analysts are employed in:
International trade and economic development agencies
Opportunities are particularly strong in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Quebec, and federal government roles in Ottawa.
Strong demand in public policy and finance
Career advancement into senior advisory roles
Opportunity to influence national economic strategy
Professionals with expertise in data analytics, econometrics, international trade, or energy economics are highly valued.
